strategies.name: Crypto 24/7 Breakout
rules: UNIVERSE: BTC, ETH, SOL (one position max per coin, equity split 3 ways).

ENTRY: 1h close breaks above the prior N-hour high (rolling high excludes the signal bar) → buy at the NEXT hour's open. Crypto trends are fat-tailed; time-series momentum is its best-documented edge (Liu & Tsyvinski 2021).
SIZING: volatility-targeted — sleeve size × clip(target_vol / realized_vol), so positions shrink automatically when the market goes wild (Moreira-Muir).
EXIT: ratcheting trailing stop (rises with each close, never falls) · hard stop below entry · optional max-hold timeout. No profit target — let trends run.
WEEKEND: Sat/Sun UTC volume is 30-50% thinner with more fakeouts, so weekend handling (trade normal / half size / no new entries) is a TESTED grid dimension, not an assumption.
COSTS: 0.10% taker fee + 0.05% slippage each side (Binance-spot-like).
strategies.desc: Strategy C — BTC/ETH/SOL trend breakout on 1-hour bars, trading around the clock (weekends included, sized per the tested weekend mode). Backtested on ~2 YEARS of true hourly data (crypto's 24/7 market gives 17k+ bars — far deeper than the 60-day equity intraday limit). Long-only spot, 0.10% fees + slippage per side, vol-targeted sizing. Params auto-tuned by the grid (config/strategyC_params.json).

strategies.name: Gap & Go Day-Trade
rules: ✅ TRUE 15-MIN VALIDATION (60d, real rules): 40 trades · 55% win · PF 1.71 · max DD 1.23% · exits 36 close / 4 stop — independently matches Humbled Trader's published 54.6% win / PF 1.59. The strategy is REAL.

⚠ The daily-bar backtest on THIS card is invalid for this strategy (it can't see intraday sequencing — the conservative stop-first rule kills entries that actually came after the morning low). Ignore its numbers.

DEPLOYMENT PATH: real-time scanner pipeline (premarket gappers → 10am+ breakout check → Telegram alert), forward-tested on paper before capital. Longer backtests need paid minute-bar history (e.g. Polygon.io).
UNIVERSE: NASDAQ-100 constituents (survivorship caveat: today's list applied across history — a free-data limitation).

SELECTION at the open: stock gapping in the signal direction with the gap and yesterday's relative volume ranking it among the top 'stocks in play' (Zarattini/Barbon/Aziz research: attention names trend intraday).
FILTERS: price ≥ $5 · 20-day dollar volume ≥ $50M · above its 50-day SMA (as of yesterday) · SPY regime gate (as of yesterday).
ENTRY: market-on-open. SIZING: fixed-fractional risk — each trade risks a set % of equity via the stop distance (research: the Sharpe engine).
EXIT (same day, always): intraday stop · optional profit target · otherwise market-on-close. 100% cash every night — no overnight gaps, which structurally caps drawdown.
HONESTY: simulated on daily bars' Open→Close window; if stop AND target both fall inside the day's range, the stop is assumed to hit FIRST (conservative). All signals knowable at entry time.
strategies.caveat: ⚠️ 呢張卡下面嘅回測數字（負Sharpe、年化~6%）唔代表真實策略，唔好被嚇親。原因：呢隻係日內策略，但免費歷史數據只有『日線』，日線睇唔到一日之內邊個時間入場出場，所以模擬保守地當你每次都食晒最差時序。用返真正嘅『15分鐘』數據驗證（原汁原味規則）：勝率 55%、盈利因子 1.71，同 Humbled Trader 公開嘅 54.6% / 1.59 幾乎一樣。即係：策略本身有效，但『免費長歷史回測』呢個工具証明唔到佢——要靠 paper 實盤累積真實紀錄。
strategies.desc: Strategy B 'Trend Join Long' (Humbled Trader) — SPLIT VERDICT. The daily-bar simulation shown on this card FAILS (best Sharpe ~-0.2 of 288 combos) because daily bars can't see intraday sequencing: it takes the morning dip as a stop-out even when the real entry came after it. The TRUE 15-minute validation (real PMH/HOD/10am rules, last 60d) PASSED: 55% win, profit factor 1.71, max DD 1.2% — matching Humbled Trader's published 54.6%/1.59. Conclusion: the strategy is real but needs intraday data; it goes live via the real-time scanner pipeline, not this daily engine. NOT deployed here by design.

strategies.name: Conviction Trend
rules: Entry: composite score ≥ 80 AND price above its 200-day SMA (up-trend only).
Ranking: on any day, the highest-scoring names fill open slots first.
Sizing: equal-weight, up to 8 positions at once, filled at next day's open.
Exit (whichever hits first): +25% take-profit · −8% stop-loss · score falls below 45 · or 30 trading days elapse.
No market filter — stays fully invested in up-trending names even in a weak tape.
strategies.desc: Only the strongest names, wide 25%/8% band, 30-day hold, fewer slots.

params._bias_note: false = backtest score is 100% technical (point-in-time clean). yfinance fundamentals are a today-snapshot and would leak future info into history.

strategies.name: Regime-Guarded
rules: MARKET GATE: only open new longs when SPY is above its own 200-day SMA. In a market down-trend it stops buying and lets existing positions run off to cash.
Entry: composite score ≥ 80 AND price above its 200-day SMA.
Diversification: at most 5 names from any single sector at once.
Sizing: equal-weight, up to 10 positions, filled at next day's open.
Exit (whichever hits first): +25% take-profit · −8% stop-loss · score below 45 · or 30 trading days.
strategies.desc: V3's conviction engine + a market-regime filter: no new longs while SPY is below its 200-SMA (sits out bear markets), light ≤5-per-sector cap. The hand-tuned baseline that V5 then grid-optimized.

params._bias_note: false = backtest score is 100% technical (point-in-time clean). yfinance fundamentals are a today-snapshot and would leak future info into history.

strategies.name: Alpha Momentum
rules: UNIVERSE: only the 28 high-beta movers (NVDA, MU, APP, MSTR, COIN...) — low-beta names like KO/MCD can't feed a momentum engine.
SCORE: 100% technical, recomputed daily from OHLC — fundamentals are NOT used in the backtest at all (a today-snapshot would be look-ahead bias).
MARKET GATE: new longs only while SPY is above its 200-day SMA.
Entry: technical score ≥ 75 AND stock above its own 200-day SMA; highest score fills just 5 concentrated slots — conviction over diversification.
Sizing: inverse-volatility — calm names get up to 1.5x the base slug, wild ones as little as 0.5x (Moreira & Muir vol-managed sizing).

Exit: NO fixed take-profit — a 10% trailing stop ratchets up under the price and locks profit ('let winners run') · 10% hard stop below entry · score decay below 45 · 20-day max hold keeps the book rotating into fresh momentum.
Robustness: the neighbouring grid point (threshold 70) also scores Sharpe 2.04 — the result is a stable parameter region, not a single lucky cell.
strategies.desc: PRODUCTION — grid winner of 288 combos on the 28-name high-beta pool: Sharpe 2.05, CAGR 67.9%, DD 14.5%, Calmar 4.68 (2023→now). Pure point-in-time technical score (zero fundamental look-ahead), 5 concentrated slots on a fast 20-day rotation, 10% trailing stop, inverse-volatility sizing, SPY-200SMA regime gate.

params._bias_note: false = backtest score is 100% technical (point-in-time clean). yfinance fundamentals are a today-snapshot and would leak future info into history.

strategies.name: Alpha Momentum · Half-Hedged
rules: IDENTICAL long book to V6 (same 28-name high-beta pool, same 100%-technical score ≥ 75 entry, 5 slots, inverse-vol sizing, 10% trailing stop, 20-day rotation, SPY-200SMA regime gate).

HEDGE: short SPY sized to 1.0 x the invested notional, marked daily and rebalanced to exposure — cancels most of the market-direction P&L so the sleeve keeps mainly the stock-selection (alpha) component.
CARRY: a 1%/yr financing/borrow drag is charged on the short notional so the hedge isn't modelled as free.

WHY: a long-only momentum book is a bull-market vehicle — it mirrors the market in a downturn. Neutralising the beta is the researched, fund-style way to stay flat-ish through a bear (see the V7 vs V6 stress comparison).
TRADE-OFF: lower beta means much of the spectacular bull-run upside is hedged away. Judge V7 on drawdown and Sharpe, not headline return.
strategies.desc: V6's exact long book PLUS a HALF market-hedge: short SPY at 0.5x the invested notional, marked daily, 1%/yr carry. The chosen all-weather compromise (a full 1.0x hedge over-protects and drags the bull too hard; a regime-only hedge protects too late because the 200SMA signal lags the top). Stress-tested: 2022 bear -11.3% / DD 11.7% (vs V6 -18% / 19%); 2023-26 bull +470% / DD 15.9% / Sharpe 2.00 (vs V6 +518% / 14.4% / 2.06). Same alpha, keeps most of the bull, halves the bear pain. This is the primary deployed to the live forward test alongside V6.

params._bias_note: false = backtest score is 100% technical (point-in-time clean). yfinance fundamentals are a today-snapshot and would leak future info into history.

strategies.name: Optimized
rules: MARKET GATE: only open new longs while SPY is above its own 200-day SMA (stands aside in bear markets, existing positions run off to cash).
Entry: composite score ≥ 80 AND price above its 200-day SMA.
Diversification: at most 5 names from any single sector.
Sizing: equal-weight, up to 10 positions, filled at next day's open.
Exit (whichever first): +20% take-profit · −10% stop-loss · score below 45 · or 45 trading days. The wider −10% stop lets winners breathe → 57% win rate.
Chosen by the optimizer for best Calmar (return per unit of drawdown), the balanced pick over a max-return or min-drawdown extreme.
strategies.desc: Strategy A PRODUCTION build — grid-search winner of 192 combos, picked for best return-per-drawdown (Calmar 2.43) and highest win rate (57%). V4's regime-guarded engine, tuned to +20%/−10% exits on a 45-day hold.

params._bias_note: false = backtest score is 100% technical (point-in-time clean). yfinance fundamentals are a today-snapshot and would leak future info into history.

week.title: Short-term momentum & breakout proximity
week.criteria: Ranked mainly by 5- and 10-day price momentum, distance to the 20-day high, MACD histogram, and a healthy (not overbought) RSI. Catches names pushing hard RIGHT NOW.

month.title: Highest composite score, confirmed up-trend
month.criteria: Ranked by the full composite score (65% technical + 35% fundamental) with a bonus for trading above the 200-day SMA. The same signal Strategy A trades on.

quarter.title: Durable trend + fundamental quality
quarter.criteria: Ranked by fundamentals (EPS/revenue growth, margins, PEG), 3-month trend and the 50/200 golden cross. Favours names you can hold through noise.

methodology.note: These are NOT individually back-tested trades — they are the CURRENT scored universe re-ranked through three different time-horizon lenses. A name can top the 1-week list on a hot momentum burst yet miss the 1-month list because its overall composite/trend is weaker (and vice-versa). Strategy A/B actually back-test the entry/exit rules; this page is a live idea shortlist.
